title: Adversarial-validation weighting for train-test drift
summary: The train/test split likely shifts by driver mix, season, and race composition.
rationale: The train/test split likely shifts by driver mix, season, and race composition. A strong local CV can still miss leaderboard behavior if it overfits source-heavy regions. Covariate-shift literature supports importance weighting when the test covariates differ from train.
implementation_hint: Train a classifier to distinguish train vs test using only covariates. Convert its OOF probabilities into clipped importance weights, then retrain the main GBDT/CatBoost with those weights or use them only for model selection/blend weighting. Validate with grouped folds by `Year_Race`, and compare weighted vs unweighted models on both standard CV and year-held-out CV.
expected_effect: Small standalone AUC gain or, more likely, better public/private robustness and fewer CV-to-LB disappointments.
risk: Noisy weights can increase variance; all adversarial scores and weights must be OOF to avoid leakage, and extreme weights should be clipped.
